text:
The anon research accounts on this app are, in their aggregate, probably the most valuable thing I’ve received from the internet (outside of maybe AMZN same day delivery and QQQ-driven 401k growth)

I’ll have a question about some arcane matter related to photonics or ODC supply chain and someone will have written like 10k words on it a month ago. Ridiculous. Also humbling. You think you’re at the bleeding edge of something and boom there’s just a whole community of people who have spent a year on it

I only wish there were equivalently outspoken AI researchers (not market analysts) who opined in equivalent public detail on technical research topics

text:
4. $AEHR at $60 - The AI Chip Quality Gatekeeper

Every AI chip needs to be tested and burned in before it ships. Aehr Test Systems is the market leader in wafer-level burn-in for AI processors, silicon photonics, and power semiconductors. Just reported $37.2M in quarterly bookings at a 3.5x book-to-bill ratio. Backlog of $50.9M. Expanding into burn-in for GPUs, AI accelerators, and optical transceivers for data center interconnects. The more chips get built, the more testing they need.

text: TSEM (~$172) is your best risk-adjusted play. They’re the foundry making silicon photonics chips for everyone — COHR, LITE, and others all rely on them. SiPho revenue doubled from $106M to $228M in one year. Gross margins expanding from 20% to 27% in four quarters. $920M CapEx buildout underway. Profitable, $1.97 EPS, guiding 15% revenue growth. They win regardless of which transceiver company takes share. The valuation is stretched at ~87x trailing P/E but forward P/E drops to 30-35x as earnings ramp. Analyst target of $162 is actually below current price though, so the near-term upside is more of a 2027+ story as capacity comes online.

text:
If a whistleblower with journalistic and photographic credentials is consistently documenting anomalies in the sky, could it be that they wanted me to be the one to advance the alien narrative? What they didn’t anticipate, however, was my deep dive into photonics—triggered by a land dispute with a photonics expert who holds a personal vendetta. To project light, there has to be a source. So, could the jet at the top right corner of this photo be the very source of that projection? And if so, is it possible that defense contractors are pushing the alien narrative by projecting these images? After all, who stands to benefit most from this story? Follow the money.  

Does it strike you as odd that the dark anomaly in the sky is almost dead center of the photo I shot of the Seattle skyline? It stands to reason, the projection was framed for my shot. 

Tonia Allen-Gould

Silicon photonics stocks, including semi wafer suppliers for raw materials like InP, are soaring. Long term investors should be cautious here. 
$AXTI $IQE $COHR $LITE $AVGO

text:
CRDO was far too oversold on the initial photonics panic and we have remained bullish through it all, adding our position back into the decline.

Today Credo announced the acquisition of DustPhotonics, an Israeli-based pioneer in Silicon Photonics. This $750 million+ land grab is designed to transform Credo from a DSP/SerDes component seller into an Optical Powerhouse capable of competing directly with the Broadcom-Marvell duopoly in the 1.6T and 3.2T eras. By acquiring DustPhotonics, they are bringing Photonic Integrated Circuit (PIC) design in-house. DustPhotonics PICs integrate lasers, modulators, and detectors onto a single silicon chip & their roadmap extends directly to 3.2T Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) and Near-Port Optics (NPO). This is a vertical integration move, as previously, Credo had to source SiPho PICs from third parties, creating a margin and supply chain dependency.

Credo is up an eye watering 24% today including the move after the close.

text:
$CRDO
Credo: to acquire DustPhotonics in $750M optical connectivity deal:
- Deal valued at $750M plus stock consideration 
- Includes 0.92M shares and up to 3.21M earnout shares - Expands capabilities in silicon photonics and high-speed connectivity 
- DustPhotonics supports 400G, 800G, 1.6T and future 3.2T solutions 
- Combined optical segment projected to exceed $500M revenue by FY2027 
- Enhances exposure to AI data center networking demand 
- Target operates fabless with ~70 employees

$CRDO 

     Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd. is a semiconductor connectivity company focused on high-speed data infrastructure, particularly for artificial intelligence data centers, hyperscale cloud customers, and advanced networking systems.

     I like the company a lot, https://t.co/4t5JAh9qNX

text:
Samsung Foundry just entered the silicon photonics market.
300mm platform. PDK ready. 2029 CPO turnkey roadmap.
Device performance? Competitive.
MRM 74GHz. MZM VpiL 1.4V·cm.
But here is the problem.
Zero customers. Zero production history.
TSMC COUPE is already in 2026 production qualification.
NVIDIA Spectrum-X confirmed.
The gap: roughly 3 years.
And here is the part most investors are missing.
The foundry generating the most SiPh revenue today is not TSMC.
Q4 run rate: $380M/year.
5x capacity expansion underway. 70% pre-booked with customer prepayments.
Who it is, why they are hard to beat, and whether Samsung's vertical integration card actually works.
Full analysis inside.
$TSM $GFS $TSEM $NVDA $AVGO

text:
POET Technologies is squarely in the FUTURE column. Their Optical Interposer platform integrates photonics + electronics on silicon (CMOS-style), enabling exactly what the diagram shows: light built into GPU packages, lower power, more chiplets, no external lasers wasting energy, and faster AI data center interconnects.

They're past pure R&D—$5M+ production orders in, 30k+ optical engines shipping 2026, 800G engines Q3, 1.6T prototypes late 2026. Revenue ramp starts now, but full hyperscale scale is 2027+. Solid play on the shift.

This company was always overlooked due to photonics fear. Photonics could never eat their business.  

Too central a position in the infrastructure space and too much cash flow with increasing margins. 

Now with photonics arm integrated they already have the customers and distribution, if customers demand photonics they have the trust and scale to deliver. 

The floor here is incredibly high and the ceiling just got raised. One of the safest bets in the market.

text:
The shift to Glass Substrates and Co-Packaged Optics is the biggest infrastructure pivot in a decade. If you trade AI, semicap, or photonics, this is required reading.

https://t.co/HjI1pTeIX0

$LPKF is interesting because it sits at the enabling layer of this transition. Its LIDE and glass-substrate systems are designed for defect-free through-glass vials, precise glass structuring, and production-scale advanced packaging, exactly the sort of process capability glass-core substrates need if they are going to matter in AI data centers and heterogeneous integration.

What makes this different from a normal “new tech” story is that the entire stack is moving at once. Hyperscalers want lower power, higher bandwidth, and tighter integration, and that pushes the industry toward optical I/O, co-packaged optics, and glass-based interposers that can support cleaner signal paths and better manufacturing scalability.

$LPKF is the machinery story. It is the precision laser-processing and glass-structuring pick that benefits if glass substrates become a standard platform rather than a niche experiment. The market often prices the chip designers and optics vendors first, but the equipment and process layer can be just as important once adoption starts.

Then you have the broader photonics names that represent different parts of the buildout. Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE operates in Wireless and Photonics, with photonics products tied to optical communication, sensing, and optical wireless networks, so it is exposed to the same bandwidth and light-based connectivity trend that supports CPO.

$IQE is another name that matters because it manufactures compound semiconductor materials and epitaxial wafers for photonics applications, including visible and infrared materials used in sensing and communications. That makes IQE part of the upstream materials layer that optical systems depend on, especially where compound semiconductors and III-V materials are central.

$POET is a more direct photonic-integration story because it is built around the POET Optical Interposer, a platform meant to integrate electronic and photonic devices into a single multi-chip module using wafer-level manufacturing and packaging. That positions POET squarely inside the AI/datacenter optical-connectivity narrative rather than adjacent to it.

$LITE and $COHR are the more established photonics names, and they matter because they make lasers, optical subsystems, and photonic technologies that power AI and cloud infrastructure. Nvidia’s recent strategic investment in both companies underscores how serious the industry is about securing next-generation optical supply for AI infrastructure.

text: @eyesigh558 Mobile is 52% of revenue and was down 36% year over year last quarter. That is exactly why the stock is down 75% from its highs and why the photonics segment is being ignored. The mobile downcycle is the noise

People are shifting to majors in $AMZN $GOOG $NVDA and also in photonics they are moving towards the majors... $CLS $COHR and $CREDO 

You can see it. People are rushing to secure gains in photonics but don't want to leave the sector of semi's. 

High beta options might struggle that have ran 100-200%+

$ALAB and $APH still have room.

text:
(2/3) Why Photonics?

Traditional chips move data with electrons through copper wires. It worked… until AI exploded. Now hyperscalers are moving insane amounts of data between thousands of GPUs every single second. Copper simply can’t keep up: 
→ Too slow 
→ Too hot 
→ Way too power-hungry

Photonics fixes everything. It uses light (lasers + optical fibers) instead of electricity → 10-100x faster data movement, dramatically lower power use, and way less heat.

March 2026 was the wake-up call: $NVIDIA dropped $4 BILLION into the optics supply chain. The “copper wall” is now the #1 bottleneck for agentic AI.

Market already exploding: AI cluster optics jumping from $16.5B → $26B in 2026 alone (60% growth in one year).

text:
AI's $1T photonics bottleneck isn't lasers. 

It's testing them.

When I attended $NVDA GTC, a comment from the CTO of $CRWV at a niche side event stuck with me.

“If there’s a day where I don’t have to hear about failed optics, I will be so happy."

This brought me down a rabbit hole. And what I found completely changed how I think about the AI trade. 

Photons are unreliable and optics failures are costing data centers billions of dollars.

Before any GPU cluster goes live every optical component has to pass brutal, failure proof testing. One bad laser in a 100,000 GPU cluster isn’t a bug. It’s a multi-million dollar outage.

Now optical testing isn’t a side market. It’s going from 19% → 30% of the entire photonics stack this decade. It's growing faster than the optics themselves!

This means the real leverage in the photonics trade might not be in the laser makers themselves but in the gatekeepers signing off on every photon.

I allocated 20% of my $3M fund tracking photonics stocks to two stocks positioned to outperform as the optics guardians.

To explain the thesis, @KawzInvests and I just dropped our photonics testing deep dive on Substack. Link in bio or below.

$VIAV $AEHR $COHR $LITE $AAOI $CIEN $TSEM

When I attended $NVDA GTC, a comment from the CTO of $CRWV at a niche side event stuck with me. “If there’s a day where I don’t have to hear about failed optics, I will be so happy." This brought me down a rabbit hole. And what I found completely changed how I think about the AI trade. Photons are unreliable and optics failures are costing data centers billions of dollars. Before any GPU cluster goes live every optical component has to pass brutal, failure proof testing. One bad laser in a 100,000 GPU cluster isn’t a bug. It’s a multi-million dollar outage. Now optical testing isn’t a side market. It’s going from 19% → 30% of the entire photonics stack this decade. It's growing faster than the optics themselves! This means the real leverage in the photonics trade might not be in the laser makers themselves but in the gatekeepers signing off on every photon. I allocated 20% of my $3M fund tracking photonics stocks to two stocks positioned to outperform as the optics guardians. To explain the thesis, @KawzInvests and I just dropped our photonics testing deep dive on Substack. The AI Photonics & Optical Infrastructure sector is showing strong bullish momentum, led by $AAOI's +9.48% surge. AAOI announced a $71M follow-on 800G trans EBC Financial Groupceiver order on April 2, bringing total hyperscale customer orders to $124M since mid-March, stacking on top of the earlier $200M 1.6T order. That backlog keeps compounding and the stock is running on pure order flow validation (https://t.co/usmdBvTq0A).
The broader sector is riding the 800G-to-1.6T transceiver migration cycle hard, with InP substrate names $AXTI (+8.26%) and $IQE (+8.05%) catching a sympathy bid as the supply chain bottleneck thesis gets repriced higher. Confidence across the optical stack is broad today, with 17 of 23 names green and only the smaller/OTC names lagging on their usual liquidity friction.

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
AAOI, LITE, CIEN, COHR, FN, GLW: The transceiver stack is the clear leader today. AAOI's order cadence is accelerating and the market is pricing in a multi-quarter 800G/1.6T revenue cycle, not a one-off. LITE continues to benefit from the Nvidia strategic investment halo and InP laser capacity expansion. CIEN and COHR are drafting behind the same hyperscaler capex tailwind. FN and GLW are steadier plays on the picks-and-shovels side of the optical build.
2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
AXTI, IQE, TSEM, GFS: AXTI and IQE are running together again as the InP substrate bottleneck thesis gets fresh validation from every new transceiver order announcement downstream. The supply chain math is simple: more 800G/1.6T orders means more InP wafer demand, and these two are the primary merchant suppliers. TSEM and GFS are quieter but green, riding foundry utilization expectations.
3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
LWLG, POET, ALMU, SIVEF, FPLSF, BESIY: LWLG is the standout here at +4.32%, likely catching speculative flow as EO polymer interest picks up alongside the transceiver cycle. The rest of this group is red and that is the usual story: OTC liquidity constraints, dilution overhang, and thin float dynamics keep these names from participating in sector-wide rallies until the bid gets aggressive enough to clear the spread.

$GLW really solid trade on this yesterday, 150C hit 14+ from entry in the 2s🔥

- leading stock in a leading theme (photonics)

- extreme relative strength vs SPX at the time - if you go back to the intraday chart of GLW and SPX it's pretty evident as SPX was selling and near the lows GLW was acting like a beach ball under water waiting to explode and make new highs 
- the trade was already in profit by end of day yesterday but the market wide gap up today was the obvious cherry on top

text:
Boston Micro Fabrication (@bmf3d), announced the launch of the BMF Clear, an optically transparent photopolymer resin engineered for applications requiring exceptional light transmission and micron-level accuracy, such as microfluidics, photonics, advanced optical components, biomedical devices and more.

With greater than 90% light transmittance, this breakthrough material enables engineers and innovators to additively manufacture complex, internally structured micro-scale devices that were previously unachievable with 3D printing. 

@SME_MFG #3DPrinting #AdditiveManufacturing
https://t.co/Z9AfhyDCOC

$SPY / $QQQ
The best buying opportunity, I believe, will be in October for the indexes. Individual stocks are uncertain; it depends on thematics, as the photonics and memory storage stocks are currently in a parabolic/hyperscale phase. We’ll likely see investments keep pouring into these as a theme into 2026. If you study the price action of the S&P 500 since the 1950s. The market typically struggles during midterm years with an average return of -16.1% (1Y). Forward return avg is +39%.

Trump's 2018 Midterm year "backtest results "
Topped in Jan—11.8% sell but continued to make a new high in sept then crashed 20%, and 1-yr return was 35%

War resolved or not, I personally think it' still lower; we could make a new ATH like 2018, but I would expect a 20% drop which historically only has ever happened in most extreme economic collapses

I'll leave this here and reflect back towards the end of the year; if I'm wrong, the market will make new highs. Won't delete this tweet.

Optics, Photonics, Optical Networking has been in a super cycle since last October

First the obvious winners ran $COHR, $CIEN, $LITE, $AAOI.
Then to second order suppliers $TSEM, $AXTI, $AEHR.
The flow:
leaders → suppliers → test/tools

A  next step in that chain not the obvious end-market winner, could be AI optical interconnects as the theme moves deeper into the stack

MACOM $MTSI sits in that bucket

text:
Most photonics stocks dipped amid early Iran war volatility (Feb-Mar 2026) due to oil spikes, risk-off flows, and supply fears—tech/semicon sector down ~8% overall.

Post-April 8 ceasefire, AI/optical plays like NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, and ANET rallied hardest on energy relief and trade optimism. 

More resilient: CSCO, GLW (stable networking/materials), KEYS/ONTO (test gear with defense ties). HIMX/LITE took bigger hits from chain disruptions. 

Sector poised for rebound if talks hold. Not advice—DYOR.

Both are high-risk plays in silicon photonics for AI data centers—no clear "winner" as they're pre-revenue and execution-dependent. 

$LWLG: Strong on polymer modulators, recent Tower/GF foundry deals, tier-1 customer pipeline (Stage 3), funded to 2027. Stock's run hot (new 52w highs ~$8-10).

$POET: Optical interposer/hybrid lasers closer to scale—Q2 2026 production ramp, $430M cash, LITEON partnership, OFC buzz. Recent Q4 results drove 15%+ weekly gains.

I'd lean $POET for nearer-term revenue catalysts if they hit targets; $LWLG for longer-term tech edge. DYOR, NFA.

As it stands:

1. ChatGPT -> 24% ( $AEHR +83%)
2. Grok -> +19% ( $AAOI + 37%)
3. Claude -> +17% 
4. Gemini -> +16%

They are all comfortably beating the market. Photonics are not showing any signs of slowing down.

text:
⚡️⚡️⚡️⚡️

The report from Lumentum ($LITE) CEO Michael Hurlston has sent shockwaves through the market, confirming that the bottleneck has shifted from "making the chips" to "connecting the chips." With $LITE basically booked through 2028, the industry is in a massive "Photonics Panic."
If you’re looking at that list for the most impactful plays right now, here are the top 5 tickers based on current market dynamics and recent orders:

1. Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI) 🌟🌟🌟🌟
This is the immediate "overflow" play. Just this week, they disclosed a massive $200 million+ order from a major cloud provider (likely Microsoft) for their 1.6T transceivers. As $LITE capacity vanishes, $AAOI is proving it can handle the spillover with its Texas manufacturing lines. They are no longer just a "Plan B"; they are becoming a primary lead for the next generation of AI networking.

2. Coherent ($COHR) ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
While $LITE is getting the headlines for being "sold out," Coherent is their fiercest rival and just received a $2 billion R&D investment from Nvidia. They hold the keys to EML (Electro-absorption Modulated Laser) technology, which is the gold standard for high-performance links. If $LITE can't take orders, $COHR becomes the default gatekeeper of the industry.

3. Fabrinet ($FN) 🌟🌟🌟🌟
If you want to own the "factory" for the entire sector, this is it. They physically build the modules for Nvidia, Cisco, and even Lumentum. They are currently expanding with a massive 2 million square foot facility in Thailand specifically to handle the 800G and 1.6T ramp-up. As long as the demand for light exists, $FN is the one getting paid to assemble it.

4. Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
This is a high-alpha "Quality Gatekeeper" play. When clusters cost billions, you can't afford a single laser to burn out. $AEHR just reported a record book-to-bill ratio of 3.5x, driven by demand for their FOX-XP systems that test silicon photonics at the wafer level. Their stock recently jumped over 25% following a surge in AI-related bookings, making them a major winner in the "yield matters most" phase.

5. Marvell $MRVL 🌟🌟🌟🌟
Hardware is just glass and metal without the brains. Marvell’s DSP (Digital Signal Processor) silicon is mandatory for these lasers to communicate with GPUs. While Broadcom is a giant, Marvell is often viewed as the "purer" play for AI data center infrastructure. Nvidia’s recent signals have been a huge tailwind for them, as every 1.6T module sold requires a Marvell chip inside.

The Bottom Line:
The speculative phase is over. We’ve entered the "Brutal Execution" phase. If you're betting on a "re-rating," $AAOI and $AEHR are showing the most explosive momentum right now because they are picking up the massive slack left by the $LITE capacity crunch.

text:
24 hours ago I dropped the full Photonics supply chain map.

Most of the names I highlighted have already ripped higher - some extremely hard.

Not bad for “just optics”.

But here’s the part the market still doesn’t fully get:

Almost everything in photonics has run up… yet not all of it equally.

For many names valuations are now very stretched.

Yet not all of them are in the same place.

Some are trading at premium multiples with strong momentum.

Others are still in early stages with improving fundamentals.

And a few remain almost completely under the radar - despite sitting on massive asymmetric upside for the next leg of the AI supercycle.

So tell me honestly:

Which photonics play do YOU believe still has the biggest upside left right now?

Drop your $TICKER + one-sentence thesis below 👇

I read every reply.

The AI industry has officially shifted from a "Compute Crunch" to a "Photonics Panic." 

$LITE CEO Michael Hurlston just delivered a seismic warning: if current order rates for US hyperscalers persist for just two more quarters, their production capacity will be completely sold out through the end of 2028.

This isn't just a corporate milestone; it’s a structural ceiling for AI growth. 

When the market leader closes its books for the next three years, the "Optical Nervous System" of AI becomes the most valuable commodity on Earth.

1⃣The Direct Capacity Alternatives (Primary Laser & Module Sources)

As hyperscalers scramble for slots that Lumentum can no longer provide, they are diverting billions to the only other firms with high-volume production lines:

➡️Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI: 

The primary beneficiary of the spillover. Their strategic partnership with Microsoft and massive Texas-based manufacturing capacity makes them the immediate "Plan B" for 800G and 1.6T modules.

➡️Coherent $COHR: 

Lumentum’s most formidable rival. They hold a massive share in EML (Electro-absorption Modulated Laser) technology. 

With LITE full, Coherent becomes the default gatekeeper for high-end optical capacity.

2⃣The Foundry & OSAT Layer (Manufacturing & Advanced Packaging)

These firms provide the physical fabrication and specialized assembly services for the industry's biggest players:

➡️Tower Semiconductor $TSEM: 

A crucial "Foundry" player. They specialize in Silicon Photonics (SiPh) fabrication, acting as the factory floor for fabless designers who need to integrate light onto silicon at scale.

➡️Fabrinet $FN: 

The "Gold Standard" of optical contract manufacturing. 

They physically assemble the complex modules for Nvidia, Cisco, and Lumentum. 

A sold-out industry means Fabrinet’s high-precision lines are the most contested real estate in tech.

3⃣The "Intelligence" Layer (DSP & Architecture Control)

Hardware is useless without the silicon that manages the signals. 

This layer dictates the efficiency of every photon:

➡️Marvell $MRVL & Broadcom $AVGO: 

The duopoly in DSP (Digital Signal Processors). Every laser module requires their silicon to "talk" to the GPU.

Marvell’s TERA platform is the mandatory brain behind the 1.6T era.

➡️MACOM Technology $MTSI: 

The pioneer of LPO (Linear Pluggable Optics). By removing the power-hungry DSP in specific short-reach links, MACOM offers a "power-saving" escape hatch for data centers hitting their electricity grid limits.

4⃣The Innovation Accelerators (Bridging the Supply Gap)

With traditional capacity blocked, these innovators are accelerating "Next-Gen" architectures to bypass the bottleneck:

➡️Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE: 

A leader in external light sources (CW-WDM) for CPO (Co-Packaged Optics). 

Sivers is essential for moving the laser from the pluggable module directly into the processor package - the holy grail of efficiency.

➡️Alumea $ALMU: 

A specialist in high-efficiency silicon photonics engines, streamlining the transition to 1.6T and 3.2T speeds where traditional optics fail.

➡️POET Technologies $POET: 

Their "Optical Interposer" is a motherboard for light, allowing for radical miniaturization and lower-cost assembly compared to traditional "active" optical alignment.

➡️Lightwave Logic $LWLG: 

Developing proprietary electro-optic polymers. These materials modulate light at speeds (200G+ per lane) that standard inorganic crystals struggle to achieve.

5⃣The Transport Giants (DCI & Global Infrastructure)

Data must move between clusters and across continents. These firms control the "Inter-City" light:

➡️Ciena $CIEN: Their WaveLogic coherent optics are the global standard for long-haul Data Center Interconnect (DCI).

➡️Nokia $NOK - Optical Networks (NOC): 

They provide the high-capacity optical transport and carrier-grade routing that form the literal backbone of the global AI internet.

6⃣The Quality Gatekeepers (Testing & Validation)

In a world of scarcity, a single "dud" laser can take down a $10B cluster. 

Yield is everything:

➡️Aehr Test Systems $AEHR: 

The kings of wafer-level "burn-in." 

Their FOX-XP systems test thousands of lasers simultaneously under extreme stress to ensure they don’t fail after installation.

➡️FormFactor $FORM: 

They provide the ultra-precise "probes" and test systems that validate optical performance on the wafer before it is even cut into chips.

7⃣The Atomic Foundation (Raw Materials & Substrates)

The "Bottleneck of Bottlenecks." 

No substrate = no laser. 

Period.

➡️AXT Inc $AXTI: 

A critical supplier of Indium Phosphide (InP) wafers—the physical medium required for the high-performance lasers that drive AI.

➡️IQE PLC $IQE: 

The masters of Epitaxy. They "grow" the complex semiconductor layers on wafers atom-by-atom. IQE is the first point of failure in the global supply chain.

➡️Soitec $SOI.PA: 

The dominant provider of SOI (Silicon-on-Insulator) wafers, the essential building block for the entire Silicon Photonics movement.

⬇️Executive Summary: The Photonics Supercycle

A situation where a market leader (Lumentum) sells out production nearly 3 years in advance happens once a decade. This means the speculative phase of AI has ended, and the phase of brutal infrastructural execution has begun.

Key Investor Takeaways:

▶️Seek "Available Capacity": If LITE is full, capital and orders will immediately flow to AAOI and COHR.

▶️Watch the Foundations: Without wafers from AXTI and processes from IQE, not a single additional laser can be built. These are the true "Gatekeepers."

▶️Bet on Quality: With such massive production scales, errors are inevitable. Testing companies like AEHR and FORM will benefit from every photon produced.

▶️Innovation is Mandatory: New architectures like LPO (MACOM) or CPO (Sivers/POET) are no longer just curiosities, they are the only way to prevent AI from "suffocating" due to energy and bandwidth limits.

⬇️Question to the Community:

Analyzing the current supply chain and the fact that optical infrastructure is becoming the new "AI bottleneck", which of these companies would you invest in today at their current market valuation?

Which one has the highest potential for a "re-rating" in the coming quarters?

Here's why I think this $6B space pioneer is an asymmetrical bet. I love $FLY because its business spans the full orbital stack: roughly 20% launch, 40% spacecraft, and 40% defense software. On the spacecraft side, $FLY’s Blue Ghost lander makes it the only company in history to land successfully on the moon. A 10 year old space startup achieved the same feat only 5 countries had before. It now has $487M in contracted NASA missions and CEO Jason Kim has said Firefly is in discussions with NASA about block buy contracts for 5 to 10 landers, which would represent between $500 million and $1.4 billion in a single order. That’s asymmetric growth against a company doing $160 million in trailing revenue. $FLY also has a spacecraft called Elytra which is this swiss army knife of sorts. It’s part tugboat, part sentinel. It launches payloads, deploys them, then stays on station for years as a comms relay, surveillance platform, or deployer. What I love about Elytra is that it’s about to monopolize lunar imaging using Nvidia Jetson compute which can create high-margin revenue to map mining and landing sites as we work to colonize the moon. Now the real leverage with $FLY is that it also builds its own rockets. It has a small lift rocket called Alpha that’s done seven launches (it’s worth noting only 3 were successful, but $RKLB’s first Electron flights were also rocky) and has a medium lift rocket Eclipse launching in early 2027 co-developed with Northrup Grumman. This timeline isn’t too far off $RKLB’s delayed Neutron vehicle now Q4 2026. The key point here is that Firefly’s spacecraft will one day own the highway on which they travel. As the launch business progresses, it’s de-risked by hundreds of millions worth of lunar landing contracts, helping boost margins on the primary spacecraft division. Vertical integration at its finest. Don’t get me wrong, $FLY is significantly behind its comparable $RKLB in the launch market. It’s a Ford to a Ferrari. But here's what many retail investors interested in space need to understand. Space companies like $RKLB and SpaceX are not being priced on their launch businesses as much as they are their higher margin divisions. $RKLB’s premium multiple is justified by their space systems division and specifically the components business (60-70% gross margins) or with SpaceX, Starlink. That’s where $FLY’s defense software becomes so important. I think it’s the primary vector on which the market is mispricing Firefly. Firefly bought a missile defense AI software company called SciTec in November that brought $164M in trailing revenue, about equal to $FLY’s entire 2025 haul and about 40% of 2026 guidance in an accretive transaction that was mostly stock at $50/share. This business is EBITDA and cash flow positive, a rarity in any space economy unit. It has a $372M FORGE contract with the Space Force and has major upside with the $185B Golden Dome initiative. If 40% of $FLY’s revenue is coming from an extremely fast growing Palantir-esque defense software company with margins that are presumably much higher than anything Rocket Lab is doing in space systems… Then why should $FLY trade at such a steep discount to $RKLB? Just 12.5x forward EV/Revenue compared to 43x for $RKLB. I think as the market better grasps the SciTec opportunity, we could see $FLY re-rate to at least half of $RKLB’s multiple, presenting major upside over the current share price. Of course there are massive risks with this idea. $FLY isn’t expected to deliver positive EPS until 2028, while comps like $RKLB and $LUNR are expected to do so in 2027. Over $800M in cash is a decent war chest but dilution will happen as the company has maxed out its credit facility, so investors should be prepared for ATMs. A shitty launch record with Alpha doesn’t instill major confidence for the launch business or the future synergies of that business on the rest of the pie, but it's still early and RKLB electron had early stage issues too. Launch commoditization is a risk here but it's mitigated by the massive pipeline of NASA lunar lander contracts if Eclipse can service some of those payloads. Private equity company AE industrial partners is also a big governance risk, with 3 board seats and majority voting control. They forced a bad acquisition on $RDW, which diluted shareholders. Finally, there’s just massive execution risks in any space business that can lead to significant delays combined with lumpy government contracting revenue that can lead to earnings misses. Overall, I think $FLY is the most complete small cap space package that the market is still sleeping on. It owns the moon, the rockets, and the software. And if the SpaceX IPO is supposed to re-rate the entire space sector, isn’t $FLY the biggest direct beneficiary since the mission is now the Moon and not Mars? It’s the space stock most primed to directly assist SpaceX with its primary IPO objective. text:
Credo (CRDO) just vaulted into AI optics with DustPhotonics, echoing Marvell’s silicon‑photonics playbook.

Credo (CRDO) advances next as DustPhotonics adds ready optics to its active cable and ZeroFlap lineup. This holds because hyperscalers now prioritize in‑rack to between‑rack optical links for faster AI cluster buildouts. Today’s strong after‑hours pop reflects the headline, while multi‑quarter design wins and module ramps remain only partly shown in price action this week. DSPs and optical modules shift from nice‑to‑have to must‑have as rack bandwidth climbs. Peer precedence from Marvell’s Celestial AI deal supports a rerate window. Memory‑to‑compute bandwidth needs keep optics at the center for AI rollouts.

text:
Credo (CRDO) just vaulted into AI optics with DustPhotonics, echoing Marvell’s silicon‑photonics playbook.

Credo (CRDO) advances next as DustPhotonics adds ready optics to its active cable and ZeroFlap lineup. This holds because hyperscalers now prioritize in‑rack to between‑rack optical links for faster AI cluster buildouts. Today’s strong after‑hours pop reflects the headline, while multi‑quarter design wins and module ramps remain only partly shown in price action this week. DSPs and optical modules shift from nice‑to‑have to must‑have as rack bandwidth climbs. Peer precedence from Marvell’s Celestial AI deal supports a rerate window. Memory‑to‑compute bandwidth needs keep optics at the center for AI rollouts.

@aleabitoreddit Hedge funds and peels?
Picked wrong.
Photonics and memory supercycles don’t whisper.
Shorts misread fundamentals, obviously.
Tangerine peel funds miss the demand.
Astounding callouts on obvious beneficiaries.
Time to rethink the trade thesis.

text:
$CRDO is acquiring DustPhotonics in a $750M silicon photonics deal that also includes stock. 

The deal expands Credo deeper into optical connectivity across 400G to 3.2T with the combined optical business expected to exceed $500M in FY2027.

BOOM 💥

$AAOI and $SNDK:

Are the perfect examples why you don’t follow hedge funds named after Tangerine peels…

Like if you’re going short, do it with companies with terrible fundamentals.

Not two of the largest beneficiaries of the photonics and memory supercycles?

I struggle to understand the reasoning behind their trades.

text:
💡Credo $CRDO 、イスラエルSiPho企業DustPhotonicsを7.5億ドルで買収 | Silicon Photonicsの垂直統合へ踏み出す

Credo Technology $CRDO が、Silicon Photonics（SiPho）Photonic Integrated Circuit（PIC）の開発企業DustPhotonicsの買収で最終合意に達した。2026年4月13日付の発表によれば、対価は現金7.5億ドルと約92万株のCredo普通株で構成され、さらに一定の財務マイルストーン達成に応じて最大約321万株の追加対価が支払われる可能性がある。クロージングは2026年第2四半期（暦年）を見込み、FY2027（Credoの会計年度）における非GAAP EPSへのアクレティブ効果を期待している。

この買収の核心は、Credoの接続ポートフォリオにおける「光」領域の垂直統合にある。同社はすでにSerDes IP、DSP、ZeroFlap（ZF）Optical Transceiver、Active Electrical Cable（AEC）といった電気・光接続の両面で製品群を構築してきたが、光トランシーバの中核部品であるSiPho PICは外部調達に依存していた。DustPhotonicsの技術を内製化することで、400G/800G/1.6T対応のPICを自社スタック内に取り込み、3.2Tまでのロードマップ拡張も視野に入る。Credoはこの統合により、外部サプライ依存の軽減、開発サイクルの短縮、量産時のコスト構造改善を見込んでいる。

DustPhotonicsはイスラエル・モディイン（Modi'in）に本社を構えるファブレス半導体企業で、2017年の創業以来、光の機能をシリコンチップ上に集積するSiPho PIC技術に注力してきた。会長のAvigdor WillenzはHabana LabsをIntelへ、Annapurna LabsをAWSへ売却した実績で知られるイスラエル半導体業界の重鎮であり、DustPhotonicsにも創業初期から投資してきた。同社は累計1億ドル超を調達しており、投資家にはGreenfield Partners、Intel Capital、Sienna Venture Capital、Exor Ventures、そしてAtreides Managementが名を連ねる。従業員数は約70名。取締役にはWRVI Capital創設者でIntel元取締役のLip-Bu Tanも参画していた。

なお、買収発表の前日にはイスラエルのCalcalistが、DustPhotonicsが数億ドル規模の売却交渉を進めており、Intel、NVIDIA $NVDA 、Amazonが候補に挙がっていると報じていた。結果的にCredoが獲得した形だが、主要テック企業が争奪戦を繰り広げていたことは、SiPho PIC技術への業界全体の関心の高さを物語っている。

市場の反応は即座だった。発表を受けて $CRDO は約12%上昇し、134ドル台に到達している。Credoの直近四半期（FQ2 2026）の売上高は約4.07億ドル、EPSは1.07ドル（予想0.91ドルに対し17.4%のサプライズ）と好調であり、年間売上成長率は前年比+201.5%を記録していた。時価総額は約199億ドル。Credoは今回の買収を含む光事業の拡大により、ZF Optical Transceiver、Optical DSP、SiPho製品を合わせた光関連売上がFY2027に5億ドルを超えると見込んでいる。

SiPho PIC市場全体については、LightCountingとCredoの推計で2030年までに60億ドル規模に成長すると予測されている。AIクラスタの規模拡大に伴い、ポート速度が800Gを超えて1.6T、3.2Tへと移行する過程で、SiPho PICは従来のプラガブルトランシーバに比べて低消費電力・低コスト・高信頼性を実現する構造的優位性を持つ。Near Port Optics（NPO）やCo-Packaged Optics（CPO）といった次世代実装形態においても、SiPho PICは基盤技術としての位置づけを強めている。

📍今後の展望
Credoのこの一手は、AI接続インフラの競争地図を塗り替える可能性を持つ。SerDes IPからDSP、SiPho PIC、光トランシーバ、AECまでを一社で提供できるフルスタック接続プラットフォームは、Broadcom $AVGO やMarvell $MRVL といった競合にはない独自のポジショニングとなる。ハイパースケーラーにとって、AIクラスタの信頼性と電力効率は最優先課題であり、Credoの垂直統合はまさにその2点に直接応える戦略である。

一方、リスクも存在する。7.5億ドルの現金支出はCredoの手元現金12.2億ドル（直近四半期末）の約6割に相当し、バランスシートへの影響は小さくない。また、70名規模のイスラエル拠点の技術チームの統合と、ファブレスモデルでの量産拡大という実行面での課題も残る。FY2027の光事業5億ドル超という強気な見通しが実現するかどうかは、2026年6月3日に予定される次回決算が最初の検証ポイントとなるだろう。

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNDM4MDYzNjE1MzI5MzYxOTM=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "💡Credo $CRDO 、イスラエルSiPho企業DustPhotonicsを7.5億ドルで買収 | Silicon Photonicsの垂直統合へ踏み出す Credo Technology $CRDO が、Silicon Photonics（SiPho）Photonic Integrated Circuit（PIC）の開発企業DustPhotonicsの買収で最終合意に達した。2026年4月13日付の発表によれば、対価は現金7.5億ドルと約92万株のCredo普通株で構成され、さらに一定の財務マイルストーン達成に応じて最大約321万株の追加対価が支払われる可能性がある。クロージングは2026年第2四半期（暦年）を見込み、FY2027（Credoの会計年度）における非GAAP EPSへのアクレティブ効果を期待している。 この買収の核心は、Credoの接続ポートフォリオにおける「光」領域の垂直統合にある。同社はすでにSerDes IP、DSP、ZeroFlap（ZF）Optical Transceiver、Active Electrical Cable（AEC）といった電気・光接続の両面で製品群を構築してきたが、光トランシーバの中核部品であるSiPho PICは外部調達に依存していた。DustPhotonicsの技術を内製化することで、400G/800G/1.6T対応のPICを自社スタック内に取り込み、3.2Tまでのロードマップ拡張も視野に入る。Credoはこの統合により、外部サプライ依存の軽減、開発サイクルの短縮、量産時のコスト構造改善を見込んでいる。 DustPhotonicsはイスラエル・モディイン（Modi'in）に本社を構えるファブレス半導体企業で、2017年の創業以来、光の機能をシリコンチップ上に集積するSiPho PIC技術に注力してきた。会長のAvigdor WillenzはHabana LabsをIntelへ、Annapurna LabsをAWSへ売却した実績で知られるイスラエル半導体業界の重鎮であり、DustPhotonicsにも創業初期から投資してきた。同社は累計1億ドル超を調達しており、投資家にはGreenfield Partners、Intel Capital、Sienna Venture Capital、Exor Ventures、そしてAtreides Managementが名を連ねる。従業員数は約70名。取締役にはWRVI Capital創設者でIntel元取締役のLip-Bu Tanも参画していた。 なお、買収発表の前日にはイスラエルのCalcalistが、DustPhotonicsが数億ドル規模の売却交渉を進めており、Intel、NVIDIA $NVDA 、Amazonが候補に挙がっていると報じていた。結果的にCredoが獲得した形だが、主要テック企業が争奪戦を繰り広げていたことは、SiPho PIC技術への業界全体の関心の高さを物語っている。 市場の反応は即座だった。発表を受けて $CRDO は約12%上昇し、134ドル台に到達している。Credoの直近四半期（FQ2 2026）の売上高は約4.07億ドル、EPSは1.07ドル（予想0.91ドルに対し17.4%のサプライズ）と好調であり、年間売上成長率は前年比+201.5%を記録していた。時価総額は約199億ドル。Credoは今回の買収を含む光事業の拡大により、ZF Optical Transceiver、Optical DSP、SiPho製品を合わせた光関連売上がFY2027に5億ドルを超えると見込んでいる。 SiPho PIC市場全体については、LightCountingとCredoの推計で2030年までに60億ドル規模に成長すると予測されている。AIクラスタの規模拡大に伴い、ポート速度が800Gを超えて1.6T、3.2Tへと移行する過程で、SiPho PICは従来のプラガブルトランシーバに比べて低消費電力・低コスト・高信頼性を実現する構造的優位性を持つ。Near Port Optics（NPO）やCo-Packaged Optics（CPO）といった次世代実装形態においても、SiPho PICは基盤技術としての位置づけを強めている。 📍今後の展望 Credoのこの一手は、AI接続インフラの競争地図を塗り替える可能性を持つ。SerDes IPからDSP、SiPho PIC、光トランシーバ、AECまでを一社で提供できるフルスタック接続プラットフォームは、Broadcom $AVGO やMarvell $MRVL といった競合にはない独自のポジショニングとなる。ハイパースケーラーにとって、AIクラスタの信頼性と電力効率は最優先課題であり、Credoの垂直統合はまさにその2点に直接応える戦略である。 一方、リスクも存在する。7.5億ドルの現金支出はCredoの手元現金12.2億ドル（直近四半期末）の約6割に相当し、バランスシートへの影響は小さくない。また、70名規模のイスラエル拠点の技術チームの統合と、ファブレスモデルでの量産拡大という実行面での課題も残る。FY2027の光事業5億ドル超という強気な見通しが実現するかどうかは、2026年6月3日に予定される次回決算が最初の検証ポイントとなるだろう。"}}}, text:
$CRDO is acquiring DustPhotonics in a $750M silicon photonics deal 

The deal structure is more complex than the headline $750M suggests:

•$750M in cash upfront
•~0.92 million CRDO shares at closing
•Up to ~3.3 million additional CRDO shares as earnout tied to specific financial milestones
•Expected close: Q2 calendar 2026, pending regulatory approval

•EPS impact: Accretive to non-GAAP EPS in fiscal year 2027
•Combined optical revenue target: Over $500M in fiscal 2027

Before this deal, Credo was a DSP (Digital Signal Processor) company that designed the brains of optical interconnects but had to source the photonics from third-party suppliers. DustPhotonics fills the exact missing layer. Post-acquisition, Credo controls the entire vertical stack

This vertical integration is critical because Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) — the next frontier of AI chip design where optics are packaged directly onto the GPU/switch die requires exactly this kind of end-to-end control. Credo now has it

Most switch ports are already at 800 Gbps and racing toward 1.6T and 3.2T.
DustPhotonics already has a 3.2T roadmap. Credo just bought years of R&D and the industry’s only 1.6T merchant silicon photonics engine.

The risk: DustPhotonics has never disclosed revenue.
$500M combined optical revenue target for FY2027 = aggressive.
The earnout structure tells you Credo knows this ,they’re hedging.

text:
CRDO가 DustPhotonics(이름이 왜 이래😊)를 인수한 내용을 Astera Labs가 aiXscale Photonics 인수한 내용과 버무려 글을 써봤습니다.

DustPhotonics의 광학 역량을 직접 확인하고 싶어서 역량껏 찾아봤는데, 학회 논문도 없고 coupling loss 수치도 미공개라 외부 검증에는 실패했습니다. 좀 아쉽지만 그 자체가 하나의 데이터 포인트라고 생각해서 솔직하게 다뤄봤습니다.

그리고 제가 생각한 진짜 수혜 레이어는 칩을 설계한 회사도, 칩을 산 회사도 아닙니다. 그 칩을 찍는 Foundry예요. 이걸 통해서 풀어봤습니다. paywall 뒤 내용인데 이 정도는 공개합니다😅

다들 CRDO 주가, 인수 사실에만 집중하던데, 공급망 구조로 읽으면 완전히 다른 회사가 보일 것입니다.

$CRDO is acquiring Israeli fabless SiPh PIC company DustPhotonics for $750M cash upfront. Up to ~$1.3B including earnout.

But the company that benefits most doesn't even appear in the headlines. $CRDO is acquiring Israeli fabless SiPh PIC company DustPhotonics for $750M cash upfront. Up to ~$1.3B including earnout. What this article covers: DustPhotonics' core L3C technology. Known specs exist, but coupling loss, alignment tolerance, and other key performance data have never been disclosed. No peer-reviewed device papers at any major conference. Not saying the tech is bad. Saying independent verification isn't possible. $ALAB acquired aiXscale Photonics in October 2025. Credo bought the PIC chip. Astera bought the fiber-chip coupling interface. Different value chain positions, same direction. Electrical connectivity companies are buying their way into optics. There's a missing variable here. DustPhotonics is fabless. They've been fabricating wafers at one specific foundry for five years. SiPh PDK switching costs are far higher than CMOS. Where does this optical internalization trend show up in the numbers first? But the company that benefits most doesn't even appear in the headlines. $CRDO is acquiring Israeli fabless SiPh PIC company DustPhotonics for $750M cash upfront. Up to ~$1.3B including earnout. What this article covers: DustPhotonics' core L3C technology. Known specs exist, but coupling loss, alignment tolerance, and other key performance data have never been disclosed. No peer-reviewed device papers at any major conference. Not saying the tech is bad. Saying independent verification isn't possible. $ALAB acquired aiXscale Photonics in October 2025. Credo bought the PIC chip. Astera bought the fiber-chip coupling interface. Different value chain positions, same direction. Electrical connectivity companies are buying their way into optics. There's a missing variable here. DustPhotonics is fabless. They've been fabricating wafers at one specific foundry for five years. SiPh PDK switching costs are far higher than CMOS. Where does this optical internalization trend show up in the numbers first? text:
Copper wires are hitting a physical wall.
As #AI models demand increases in data transfer speeds between chips, electrical resistance is becoming an insurmountable barrier.
Enter Silicon Photonics: integration of laser-driven optical comms directly onto silicon substrates. https://t.co/HmVcjDHm7G

text: Being up 50% doesn’t mean fully rerated. TSEM for example still trades at a significant discount to Western peers despite being a key player in the silicon photonics supply chain, the market hasn’t fully priced its positioning yet. Even large caps like Marvell aren’t fully rerated, the market is still pricing it as a networking chip company, not as a custom AI silicon platform. That re-rating alone is worth multiple turns on the multiple. Same logic applies to smaller names like POET, where the technology is real but institutional coverage is near zero. We’re still early on most of these

text:
Big day for Soitec! 

Up a solid 40% since we profiled it. 

A lot of the recent move is due to retail climbing into the hype around Photonics following @aleabitoreddit and @citrini being bullish on the stock.

Of all the photonics names, this probably remains my favorite reward to risk. 

Given the moat. The bombed out valuation from other segments (which will inflect soon), and the partnerships the company has with players like Shin Etsu.

Excited to hold this for longer and watch the thesis play out. 

I’m used to waiting much longer for a thesis to play out- and let it be known that we haven’t fundamentally seen it played out as of yet.

Thank you to the hive mind X community we have so many experts bringing these names to light!

I didn’t make the rules, but ima play the game to not just win, but to dominate. I only feel good when it’s a blow out.

spectroscopy? That’s photonics.

Microfluidics? Fluid dynamics and materials science. 

The detectors, the lasers, the optics, half of it traces lineage to NASA, DARPA, and physics labs.

text:
European photonics is finally waking up after all that Iran noise 🔥

$SIVE ripping today - this tiny market cap laser play supplying $MRVL, Ayar Labs and $JBL for the AI optical supercycle. 5x-10x in the next 2 years feels very doable. Crazy undervalued.

$SOI still hanging in the 50s but starting to push higher with the sector. Monopoly on SOI-substrates guys! Pay attention. This is literally right in front of your eyes.

On the US side:

$AAOI exploding after that $250M (now doubled to $500M) ATM - feeding the same 800G/1.6T wave. Great hold for the next 1-2 years.

$TSEM has been grinding up nicely for the last 3 weeks straight. Easily becoming the $TSM of the photonics boom for the next 3-4 years.

The optical bottleneck is real and these names are catching the rotation. 🚀

I called them 3 weeks ago. I’m calling it again!

Why these 3 are my photonics plays.

$SOI: Stupid low ~2.5 P/S with Western SiPh substrate monopoly - undervalued, huge pop if they execute.

$SIVE: Pure laser play for CPO/SiPh, tiny MC, Jabil + Ayar/POET ties - feels like next big rerate.

$TSEM: Safest one, foundry booked

#Photonics Spectra's People in the News for April 8:

📍Lightera appointed Holly Hulse CEO.

text:
$AEHR has been one of the most talked about names in the photonics space and after yesterday’s earnings it is not hard to see why.

Here is what $AEHR actually does and why it matters.

Every photonic wafer produced by every company at every foundry in the world has to be tested before it ships. You cannot skip this step. A faulty chip in a hyperscale data center does not just fail. 

It can take down the entire GPU cluster. $AEHR owns this step. Their FOX-XP and Sonoma systems burn-in and test wafers at scale before they ever reach a customer.

In the photonics supply chain every company in the middle competes for a socket. $COHR or $LITE wins the laser. $AVGO or $NVDA wins the switch. 

But $AEHR does not compete for anything. 

Every photonic wafer produced by every company at every foundry passes through $AEHR before it ships.

Now look at what has been happening.
February 11: Sonoma production win for a hyperscaler’s custom AI processors. Likely $GOOGL $AMZN or $META.

February 26: $14 million order from AI lead customer. Likely $AMD for Instinct MI series.
March 3: Lead silicon photonics customer orders a FOX-XP system. Likely $INTC.

March 31: Initial order from a major new silicon photonics customer. Likely $AVGO $MRVL or $CSCO transitioning to 800G and 1.6T.

Every single major name in AI is showing up on $AEHR’s order book.

Yesterday’s earnings: $37.2 million in quarterly bookings. Book-to-bill ratio over 3.5x. Record backlog of $50.9 million. Full year guidance raised to the high end of $45 to $50 million. 

Bookings expected at the high end of $60 to $80 million in the second half. CEO said AI business could reach hundreds of millions of dollars within a few years.

$2bbillion market cap. Every photonic chip in every AI data center passes through their equipment before it goes live.

The order flow is bottling up. The customers are the entire industry. The mass production orders have not even started yet.

Every photonic wafer produced by every company at every foundry passes through $AEHR before it ships. Now look at what has been happening. February 11: Sonoma production win for a hyperscaler’s custom AI processors. Likely $GOOGL $AMZN or $META. February 26: $14 million order from AI lead customer. Likely $AMD for Instinct MI series. March 3: Lead silicon photonics customer orders a FOX-XP system. Likely $INTC. March 31: Initial order from a major new silicon photonics customer. Likely $AVGO $MRVL or $CSCO transitioning to 800G and 1.6T. Every single major name in AI is showing up on $AEHR’s order book. Yesterday’s earnings: $37.2 million in quarterly bookings. Book-to-bill ratio over 3.5x. Record backlog of $50.9 million. Full year guidance raised to the high end of $45 to $50 million. Bookings expected at the high end of $60 to $80 million in the second half. CEO said AI business could reach hundreds of millions of dollars within a few years. $2bbillion market cap. Every photonic chip in every AI data center passes through their equipment before it goes live. The order flow is bottling up. The customers are the entire industry. The AI industry has officially shifted from a "Compute Crunch" to a "Photonics Panic." 

$LITE CEO Michael Hurlston just delivered a seismic warning: if current order rates for US hyperscalers persist for just two more quarters, their production capacity will be completely sold out through the end of 2028.

This isn't just a corporate milestone; it’s a structural ceiling for AI growth. 

When the market leader closes its books for the next three years, the "Optical Nervous System" of AI becomes the most valuable commodity on Earth.

1⃣The Direct Capacity Alternatives (Primary Laser & Module Sources)

As hyperscalers scramble for slots that Lumentum can no longer provide, they are diverting billions to the only other firms with high-volume production lines:

➡️Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI: 

The primary beneficiary of the spillover. Their strategic partnership with Microsoft and massive Texas-based manufacturing capacity makes them the immediate "Plan B" for 800G and 1.6T modules.

➡️Coherent $COHR: 

Lumentum’s most formidable rival. They hold a massive share in EML (Electro-absorption Modulated Laser) technology. 

With LITE full, Coherent becomes the default gatekeeper for high-end optical capacity.

2⃣The Foundry & OSAT Layer (Manufacturing & Advanced Packaging)

These firms provide the physical fabrication and specialized assembly services for the industry's biggest players:

➡️Tower Semiconductor $TSEM: 

A crucial "Foundry" player. They specialize in Silicon Photonics (SiPh) fabrication, acting as the factory floor for fabless designers who need to integrate light onto silicon at scale.

➡️Fabrinet $FN: 

The "Gold Standard" of optical contract manufacturing. 

They physically assemble the complex modules for Nvidia, Cisco, and Lumentum. 

A sold-out industry means Fabrinet’s high-precision lines are the most contested real estate in tech.

3⃣The "Intelligence" Layer (DSP & Architecture Control)

Hardware is useless without the silicon that manages the signals. 

This layer dictates the efficiency of every photon:

➡️Marvell $MRVL & Broadcom $AVGO: 

The duopoly in DSP (Digital Signal Processors). Every laser module requires their silicon to "talk" to the GPU.

Marvell’s TERA platform is the mandatory brain behind the 1.6T era.

➡️MACOM Technology $MTSI: 

The pioneer of LPO (Linear Pluggable Optics). By removing the power-hungry DSP in specific short-reach links, MACOM offers a "power-saving" escape hatch for data centers hitting their electricity grid limits.

4⃣The Innovation Accelerators (Bridging the Supply Gap)

With traditional capacity blocked, these innovators are accelerating "Next-Gen" architectures to bypass the bottleneck:

➡️Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE: 

A leader in external light sources (CW-WDM) for CPO (Co-Packaged Optics). 

Sivers is essential for moving the laser from the pluggable module directly into the processor package - the holy grail of efficiency.

➡️Alumea $ALMU: 

A specialist in high-efficiency silicon photonics engines, streamlining the transition to 1.6T and 3.2T speeds where traditional optics fail.

➡️POET Technologies $POET: 

Their "Optical Interposer" is a motherboard for light, allowing for radical miniaturization and lower-cost assembly compared to traditional "active" optical alignment.

➡️Lightwave Logic $LWLG: 

Developing proprietary electro-optic polymers. These materials modulate light at speeds (200G+ per lane) that standard inorganic crystals struggle to achieve.

5⃣The Transport Giants (DCI & Global Infrastructure)

Data must move between clusters and across continents. These firms control the "Inter-City" light:

➡️Ciena $CIEN: Their WaveLogic coherent optics are the global standard for long-haul Data Center Interconnect (DCI).

➡️Nokia $NOK - Optical Networks (NOC): 

They provide the high-capacity optical transport and carrier-grade routing that form the literal backbone of the global AI internet.

6⃣The Quality Gatekeepers (Testing & Validation)

In a world of scarcity, a single "dud" laser can take down a $10B cluster. 

Yield is everything:

➡️Aehr Test Systems $AEHR: 

The kings of wafer-level "burn-in." 

Their FOX-XP systems test thousands of lasers simultaneously under extreme stress to ensure they don’t fail after installation.

➡️FormFactor $FORM: 

They provide the ultra-precise "probes" and test systems that validate optical performance on the wafer before it is even cut into chips.

7⃣The Atomic Foundation (Raw Materials & Substrates)

The "Bottleneck of Bottlenecks." 

No substrate = no laser. 

Period.

➡️AXT Inc $AXTI: 

A critical supplier of Indium Phosphide (InP) wafers—the physical medium required for the high-performance lasers that drive AI.

➡️IQE PLC $IQE: 

The masters of Epitaxy. They "grow" the complex semiconductor layers on wafers atom-by-atom. IQE is the first point of failure in the global supply chain.

➡️Soitec $SOI.PA: 

The dominant provider of SOI (Silicon-on-Insulator) wafers, the essential building block for the entire Silicon Photonics movement.

⬇️Executive Summary: The Photonics Supercycle

A situation where a market leader (Lumentum) sells out production nearly 3 years in advance happens once a decade. This means the speculative phase of AI has ended, and the phase of brutal infrastructural execution has begun.

Key Investor Takeaways:

▶️Seek "Available Capacity": If LITE is full, capital and orders will immediately flow to AAOI and COHR.

▶️Watch the Foundations: Without wafers from AXTI and processes from IQE, not a single additional laser can be built. These are the true "Gatekeepers."

▶️Bet on Quality: With such massive production scales, errors are inevitable. Testing companies like AEHR and FORM will benefit from every photon produced.

▶️Innovation is Mandatory: New architectures like LPO (MACOM) or CPO (Sivers/POET) are no longer just curiosities, they are the only way to prevent AI from "suffocating" due to energy and bandwidth limits.

⬇️Question to the Community:

Analyzing the current supply chain and the fact that optical infrastructure is becoming the new "AI bottleneck", which of these companies would you invest in today at their current market valuation?

Which one has the highest potential for a "re-rating" in the coming quarters?

text:
oh wow $sloif .. That bodes well for $viav and $trt from @SushantJos39350 
$viav  specific benefit: Once silicon photonics chips are packaged into transceivers and installed in data centers, VIAV's field instruments and lab/production test equipment certify every optical link. VIAV benefited from an uptick in fiber deployments connecting data centers, causing demand for fiber field instruments to surge among hyperscalers and service providers. 
RCR Wireless News More Soitec wafers → more photonics chips → more optical modules → more fiber links to certify → more VIAV instruments. Additionally, VIAV's CEO highlighted the emergence of hollow core fiber and multi-core fiber — both of which require entirely new testing protocols that Soitec Photonics-SOI chips will need to interface with.
$trt  specific benefit: Trio-Tech received $5.3M in orders for burn-in boards supporting a next-generation AI GPU platform.  As silicon photonics-enabled AI clusters are deployed, the GPUs inside them are tested using TRT's burn-in boards. 
TRT's Southeast Asian footprint (Singapore, Malaysia) is also positioned directly downstream from $tsem Tower Semiconductor's silicon photonics expansion, which is concentrated in Asia. $TRT doesn't test the photonics chips directly, but it tests the AI accelerators that will be co-packaged with them.

text:
Want to know what takes the wind out of the photonics / semiconductor bubble?

This is how it starts.

China controls ~98% of global gallium production, ~68% of germanium, ~90% of rare earth refining, and dominates indium phosphide feedstock - the raw materials that every single one of these names needs to justify their insane forward P/Es.

One export restriction escalation and the entire supply chain seizes.

They’ve already done it. Gallium and germanium export controls went live in 2023. Tungsten and seven rare earths followed in 2025. 

This isn’t theoretical - it’s a playbook being actively executed.

Names most exposed to a reckoning:
$LITE trading ~200x earnings
$COHR ~250x earnings
$AAOI 112x forward P/E, negative trailing, rated “Significantly Overvalued
$LWLG pre-revenue, pure speculation

Even $FN, $MTSI, and $AXTI aren’t immune.

The market is pricing a decade of perfect execution into stocks that are one Beijing press release away from a material input crisis.

The U.S. is 100% import-reliant on gallium, germanium, indium, and tantalum for chip production. There is no domestic backup at scale. Rebuilding non-Chinese capacity would take years.

This isn’t FUD. It’s supply chain math.

“Not financial advice. This is purely our opinion.”

Basically if you look at photonics flow on $GOOGL TPU/hyperscaler ASICs kinda looks like this (very likely, but undisclosed): Optical Transceivers (highest BOM): Lumentum/Cloud Light: ~ Vital / $AXTI-> $AXTI/Sumitomo/JX -> $IQE (Epi-Wafers) -> $LITE / Cloud Light -> $FN (Contract Manufacturing) -> $GOOGL TPU Merhcant optical supply chain: ~ Vital / $AXTI -> $AXTI / Sumitomo / JX -> → $LITE / $AVGO / $COHR (EML) + $MRVL / $MTSI / Semtech -> Innolight/Eoptolink -> $GOOGL So if you want moonshot-type photonics BOM / price-hikes stocks deeper upstream in the photonics BOM: $AXTI, $IQE and your way to go. $AXTI had terrible fundamentals before but the recent Northland fundraising round cemented its run. $IQE has terrible fundamentals now (Net debt £23.5 million) but is probably one of the most critical parts of the supply chain. If they manage to sell their Taiwan operations, wouldn't be surprised if it went up quite a bit just from their inp business. There's £18m convertible notes (which is basically nothing), then there's 120 to 154m new shares (~12% to 15%), which is also kinda nothing relative to current size. On the other hand, others $LITE and Innolight are probably more established. TLDR: $IQE -> seems critical to Western supply chains, $130MC. Net debt, if they sell Taiwan business -> strong re-rating or they might just dilute you anyway. But if the Taiwan business fails to be sold, probably expect to be diluted to oblivion like Wolfspeed. So huge, huge, risk ad do you own research into risks. But $AXTI and $IQE might are personally interesting to me (I do own $IQE)."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 102, "favorite_count": 144, "quote_count": 3, "reply_count": 28, "retweet_count": 12}, "rest_id": "2026638711941902461", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "884551", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2042678057992143093", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "9", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: $LWLG might be the next photonics stock to go on a crazy run. https://t.co/0bQNMzDq5U
embedded_text:
$LWLG just signed a deal with Tower Semiconductor and the market has no idea what just happened.

Just went through an organized a list 

**Silicon Photonics (SiPh) & CPO Supply Chain**

**(Core Data Center & AI Interconnect Bottleneck)**

**Light Source (CW DFB Lasers)**
SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors)
AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics)
5802 (Sumitomo Electric)

**SiPh Foundry (SOI-based)**
TSEM (Tower Semiconductor)

**Compound Semi Foundry (III-V)**
3105 (Win Semi)

**Optical Interposers**
POET (POET Technologies)

**Substrates (SOI Wafers)**
SOI (Soitec)

**Epitaxy / Photonic Materials**
IQE (IQE plc)
2455 (Visual Photonics / VPEC)
ALMU (Aeluma)

**Epitaxy Equipment (MBE Tools)**
Riber (ALRIB)

**Advanced Packaging & OSAT (CPO Enablers)**
6451 (Shunsin Technology)
ASX (ASE Technology)

**Optical Subassembly & Packaging**
4977 (PCL Technologies)
3450 (Elite Advanced Laser)

**Fiber Arrays & Passive Interconnects**
3363 (FOCI Fiber Optic)

**Optical Modules**
COHR (Coherent)
LITE (Lumentum)

**Optical Semiconductor ICs (TIAs/Drivers)**
MTSI (MACOM)
SMTC (Semtech)

**Optical DSP & Interconnect**
CRDO (Credo Technology)
MRVL (Marvell Technology)

**Electrical Interconnect / Fabric**
ALAB (Astera Labs)

**Metrology / Inspection (Yield Control)**
268A (Rigaku Holdings Corp.)
3587 (MA-tek)
3289 (iST – Integrated Service Technology Inc.)

**Optical Test & Measurement**
6777 (Santec)

**Semiconductor Burn-in & Reliability Test**
AEHR (Aehr Test Systems)

**Speculative Modulator Materials**
LWLG (Lightwave Logic)

### **Other AI Infrastructure Bottlenecks**

**(Broader Scaling and Edge Constraints)**

**Edge AI & Enterprise Storage**
5289 (Innodisk Corp.)

**High-Power / Industrial Photonic Systems**
LASR (nLIGHT, Inc.)
6965 (Hamamatsu Photonics)

text:
$IQE $IQEPF 

Overview and bull bear debate 

Fundamental Overview

IQE is a compound semiconductor epitaxial wafer manufacturer ￼ headquartered in Cardiff, Wales. It operates through three segments: Wireless (RF devices for mobile/comms), Photonics (VCSELs, InP for sensing and datacom), and CMOS++ (advanced silicon-adjacent materials). ￼ The company serves chipmakers and OEMs from facilities in the UK, US, and Taiwan, and holds a substantial IP portfolio as a scaled epitaxy specialist. 
￼
FY2024 revenue was ~£118M, up ~2.4% YoY. ￼ FY2025 revenue came in at ~£97M — the shortfall driven by a brutal H1 where wireless revenue cratered 52% on inventory digestion and handset softness ￼, though H2 trading recovered strongly with faster-than-expected US defense funding releases and growing photonics demand from AI/datacentre. ￼ Adjusted EBITDA hit at least £2M, showing early operating leverage as utilization improved. ￼

Shares closed at ~36p on April 10, 2026, up 23% on the day ￼, with a market cap around £245M on ~979M shares outstanding. ￼ Total debt sits at ~$74M against just ~$5M cash, giving an EV of ~$176M. ￼ The 52-week range has been roughly 4.7p to 29.5p ￼ — enormous volatility, typical micro-cap.

Bull Case

•AI/datacentre photonics tailwind. IQE enters 2026 with a strong Q1 order book and improving demand visibility in datacentre and AI-related photonics. ￼ InP wafers for optical interconnects and VCSELs for 3D sensing are riding secular hyperscaler capex.

•Defense revenue acceleration. US military funding releases pulled forward into H2 2025 ￼, and GaN RF radar solutions are gaining traction in satellite comms and defense — sovereign supply chain programs in the US and Europe provide a structural demand floor.

•MicroLED optionality. IQE is developing GaN-on-Si microLEDs with consumer multinational partners for next-gen display ecosystems and high-density datacom interconnects. ￼ Management has called microLEDs the biggest potential volume driver for 8-inch compound semi. ￼

•VCSEL market leadership. IQE pioneered 6-inch and shipped the world’s first commercial 200mm VCSEL epiwafer ￼, with a patented VCSEL-on-germanium roadmap to 300mm — a meaningful moat if smartphone 3D sensing and AR/VR adoption scale.

•Operating leverage. The business is highly fixed-cost. Revenue recovery toward breakeven and beyond delivers outsized EBITDA margin expansion ￼ as fab utilization normalizes.

•Takeover/strategic review upside. IQE has explored strategic alternatives including a potential corporate sale ￼, and its IP portfolio and customer relationships make it a logical consolidation target for larger compound semi players.

Bear Case

•Balance sheet fragility. ~$74M debt against ~$5M cash ￼ leaves minimal cushion. If the recovery stalls, dilutive equity raises or distressed asset sales become real risks.

•Customer concentration. IQE is widely understood to be heavily exposed to Apple’s VCSEL supply chain. Any design-out, insourcing, or supplier diversification by Apple would be devastating.

•Cyclical whipsaw. H1 2025 wireless revenue dropped 52% YoY on inventory overhang and tariff uncertainty ￼ — the business has minimal visibility beyond a quarter or two and is at the mercy of handset OEM ordering patterns.

•Profitability still unproven at scale. Even with recovery to ~£97M revenue, adjusted EBITDA was only ~£2M ￼ — the company has been structurally unprofitable through the downturn and hasn’t demonstrated sustainable free cash flow.

•AIM liquidity risk. Sub-£250M market cap, AIM-listed, thin float — the stock can gap violently on modest volume, making position sizing and exits difficult.

•Execution risk on new platforms. MicroLED and GaN power/defense programs are still pre-revenue or early-stage. Success hinges on whether IQE can achieve operational breakeven before cash reserves are strained. ￼

Monolithic ICs are useful only in the analog space. 
SoC design is correlated with moore law. Moore law has hit a physical limit.

text:
As of April 13, 2026
The AI Photonics & Optical Infrastructure sector is showing strong bullish momentum, led by a massive surge in $ALMU. Aeluma secured over $4 million in non-dilutive U.S. government contracts to scale its heterogeneous integration platform for quantum dot lasers and AlGaAs nonlinear photonics, partnering with Tower Semiconductor and Sumitomo Chemical Advanced Technology on wafer production. (https://t.co/Pl5EKUsM55)
The III-V compound semiconductor supply chain is catching a broad bid as government and hyperscaler capital validates photonic materials as critical infrastructure. IQE ripped +32.70% on intensifying takeover speculation, while the parabolic large-caps (LITE, CIEN) digest their monster runs with healthy profit-taking.

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
CIEN, LITE: Cooling off after parabolic Q1 runs. LITE gained 90.7% in Q1 alone post-NVIDIA's $2B investment and S&P 500 inclusion. Today's pullback is position trimming, not thesis deterioration.
AAOI, FN, COHR, GLW, VIAV: FN leading at +4.19% on fiber demand. COHR flat after its own massive rally. VIAV soft.
2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
IQE: Day's standout. Lazard-advised strategic review entering final stages with multiple bidders circling the entire company and individual asset packages.
AXTI: +3.53%, structural InP supply bottleneck beneficiary as hyperscalers lock up EML capacity through 2027+.
TSEM, GFS, SOI, ATEYY, FPLSF: TSEM steady at +2.89% on SiPho foundry momentum. Rest positive but muted.
3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
ALMU: Today's catalyst leader. $4M government contract validates III-V-on-silicon integration. Non-dilutive funding is key given the active ATM shelf.
SIVEF, SIVE: Sivers ripping across both listings on InP laser positioning and Q4 2026 LiDAR production ramp catalyst.
LWLG, POET, BESIY: LWLG +7.45% catching speculative flow. POET +3.69%. BESIY fading.
4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure
FORM, AEHR: Both tracking SiPho capacity buildout. FORM +3.57%, AEHR +1.75%.
KEYS, TER, MRVL: Grinding higher on test and DSP/PAM4 demand. TER lagging at +0.58%.

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNDI3NjQ3MjA1MjkzNzkzMjk=", "text": "As of April 10, 2026

The AI Photonics & Optical Infrastructure sector is showing explosive bullish momentum, led by a 25.59% surge in $LWLG. Lightwave Logic is running on a multi-catalyst stack built since March. The core trigger is the development agreement with Tower Semiconductor, integrating LWLG's Perkinamine EO polymer modulators into Tower's PH18 silicon photonics PDK targeting 110GHz+ bandwidth for 400G-per-lane applications, with multiple engineering tapeouts scheduled through 2026. This is not a vague MOU. Tower's PH18 is a production-grade platform, and Tower itself recently disclosed a separate NVIDIA collaboration on 1.6T optical modules. LWLG also integrated its modulator into GlobalFoundries' GDSFactory design kit for 200G/400G SiPho, and completed an initial tapeout with SilTerra and Luceda Photonics with results expected mid-2026. The stock is up 764% over the past year as the market reprices LWLG from R&D-stage materials play to foundry-embedded modulator platform with a credible 2027 volume production path (https://t.co/MOtKo5Er07).

The broader value chain is bid as AI infra spending accelerates toward $690B in 2026 and the 800G-to-1.6T transceiver migration outstrips supply through mid-2027. $AAOI continues its April tear on $124M in cumulative hyperscaler orders, while $IQE and $SOI are catching parabolic bids as investors chase upstream InP exposure.

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking

AAOI, COHR, LITE, CIEN, FN, GLW: AAOI is up ~40% in April on $124M in back-to-back hyperscaler orders, targeting 500K+ units/month of 800G and 1.6T by year-end. COHR and FN grind higher on supply-constrained pricing power. LITE lags peers despite NVIDIA partnership, suggesting rotation into higher-beta names. VIAV down 2.22%, the lone red in the watchlist.

2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries

IQE, SOI, AXTI, TSEM, GFS: IQE +23.33% and SOI +19.01% as investors aggressively front-run the InP supply deficit. Every 800G/1.6T transceiver needs InP laser chips, and substrate growers are the narrowest chokepoint. AXTI +1.68%, consolidating but long-term bottleneck thesis intact. TSEM -0.11%, noise given Tower's direct role in the LWLG catalyst.

3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs

POET, SIVE, SIVEF, FPLSF, ALMU, BESIY: Speculative heat concentrated here. POET +15.79% with Foxconn-backed orders providing a revenue bridge most pre-commercial names lack. SIVE/SIVEF trading as a tandem on broad PIC enthusiasm. ALMU +0.28% remains muted on OTC liquidity friction and dilution overhang.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure

AEHR, FORM, TER, KEYS, MRVL, ATEYY: MRVL leads at +7.14% on NVIDIA partnership and custom silicon in the optical interconnect stack. AEHR +2.28% as wafer-level burn-in stays critical for compound semi qualification at volume. text:
🚨 M&A | 🟢 $CRDO Credo — Acquires DustPhotonics for $750M — Silicon Photonics Play

🔹 Credo: Acquiring DustPhotonics for $750M + stock
🔹 Speeds: 400G, 800G, 1.6T, future 3.2T
🔹 Combined optical revenue: Expected $500M+ by FY2027
🔹 DustPhotonics: Fabless, ~70 employees — pure IP acquisition

💥 Why this deal is significant:
🔹 MRVL Barclays upgrade: Optical ports doubling 2026+2027
🔹 ANET Rosenblatt upgrade: XPO optical strategy + Google/Anthropic wins
🔹 LITE: Sold out through 2028 within 2 quarters
🔹 NOW: Credo paying $750M for 70-person silicon photonics startup = optical is THAT strategic
🔹 3.2T = future standard = DustPhotonics is building the optical stack for the next generation
🔹 $500M combined optical revenue FY2027 = massive step-up for Credo

🔸 The optical supercycle M&A phase:
🔹 Credo x DustPhotonics = $750M
🔹 MRVL Barclays OW $150 (optical ports doubling)
🔹 LITE Aletheia $1,225 (sold out 2028)
🔹 SNDK Evercore $1,200 (storage optical layer)
🔹 AMAT 2nm tools for optical chip layers
🔹 Every AI chip, every data center = needs optical. M&A is how you get it fast.

📊 Watch:
🟢 $CRDO — $750M acquisition + $500M revenue FY2027 = major optical connectivity player
🟢 $LITE $MRVL $ANET — optical supercycle M&A validates the sector
🟡 Integration risk — 70 employees, all IP = talent retention = key risk

⚡ $750 million for 70 people and their photonics IP.

That’s how valuable optical connectivity has become in the AI era.

text:
Another company tying itself to multiple photonics-driven themes is $CRDO. Similar move to $MRVL.

Credo is up 9% AH following its acquisition of DustPhotonics, positioning itself into high-speed optical connectivity for AI data center interconnects. As clusters scale and bandwidth demand shifts from copper toward optics beyond the rack level, assets like DSPs and optical modules become increasingly strategic.

For comparison, Marvell is up nearly 50% since announcing its acquisition of Celestial AI, which added a silicon-photonics platform aimed at scaling memory bandwidth and chip-to-chip connectivity inside next-generation AI systems.
embedded_text: Credo $CRDO is acquiring silicon photonics company DustPhotonics for $750M in cash plus about 0.92M shares, with additional earnout shares tied to milestones. The deal should help lift its combined optical revenue to more than $500M in fiscal 2027. https://t.co/oHLu3177XL

text:
$CRDO just went from AEC king to full-stack AI connectivity platform.

Acquired DustPhotonics — now owns both the optical DSP and the silicon photonics PIC. 

That’s vertical integration of the two core components inside every optical transceiver.

What matters:

•Optical revenue expected to exceed $500M in FY27 — a net new growth engine on top of the AEC monopoly

•FY27 total revenue now guided 75%+ YoY growth

•Margins intact (mid-60s) — owning the PIC eliminates supplier margin stack

•Dust has hyperscaler design wins and a roadmap to 3.2T, ahead of the market

•Dual CPO/NPO roadmap (laser + microLED) — CRDO wins either way
The thesis just got wider. AECs for copper. Optical DSPs for networking silicon. ZeroFlap transceivers for bulletproof reliability. Standalone PICs for the $6B silicon photonics TAM by 2030.

One company. Every connection in the AI cluster. Copper to optical. Front-end to scale-up.

Top 5 Catalysts

1.June earnings — first print with DustPhotonics revenue contribution and updated FY27 guide with optical breakdown

2.DustPhotonics close (Q2 cal ’26) — removes deal uncertainty, unlocks cross-selling and integration synergies

3.ZeroFlap hyperscaler ramp — Brennan teased expanding customer traction, next update in June could name new wins

4.3.2T product cycle — Dust is ahead of market on 448 Gbps PIC, positions CRDO as the enabling platform for next-gen port speeds

5.CPO/NPO inflection — dual roadmap (laser via Dust + microLED in-house) means CRDO wins regardless of which architecture hyperscalers choose, with scale-up driving 10x connection density
