The catalyst: a definitive agreement to acquire DustPhotonics for $750M cash + stock, bringing industry-leading Silicon Photonics PIC technology in-house. This is a thesis-level event. Here’s why: The Bear Case Just Died The biggest overhang on CRDO was the copper-vs-optical debate — would optical interconnect eventually cannibalize their dominant AEC franchise? That question is now irrelevant. Credo owns both sides of the connectivity stack: AECs within racks, optical between racks and across pods. They don’t care which one wins. They win either way. What DustPhotonics Brings DustPhotonics has a differentiated SiPho PIC portfolio spanning 400G, 800G, 1.6T with a roadmap to 3.2T — already deployed at leading hyperscale AI clusters and in design for both NPO and CPO applications. This isn’t a speculative R&D bet. These are production-grade photonic integrated circuits sitting in live AI infrastructure today. The Numbers Credo now expects combined optical revenue (ZeroFlap transceivers + Optical DSPs + Silicon Photonics) to exceed $500M in fiscal 2027. For context, CRDO did ~$268M total revenue last quarter. Optical alone is on track to become a revenue pillar on par with the core AEC/IC franchise. The deal is expected accretive to non-GAAP EPS in FY27. The Vertically Integrated Moat Post-acquisition, Credo’s stack reads: SerDes → DSP → Silicon Photonics → System Integration. Electrical and optical. Scale-up and scale-out. No other pure-play connectivity company offers this full spectrum. Broadcom has optical but is a conglomerate. Marvell has optical but lacks the AEC dominance. CRDO is now the only vertically integrated connectivity pure-play purpose-built for AI datacenter fabric. The Technical Setup Price reclaimed the 50 SMA and blew through the 100 and 200 SMAs in a single session on 2x average volume. After hours puts it at $147, back inside the prior $140–165 consolidation zone from late 2025. This is conviction buying, not a dead cat bounce. Next resistance: $160–165. Conference call tomorrow, April 14 at 1 PM ET. That’s your next catalyst — integration roadmap, design win pipeline, and FY27 guidance details. When the market gives you a company that was already the AEC market leader with 75%+ share, and they go acquire the missing piece to own the entire AI connectivity stack from copper to light — you pay attention."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2043796885735956990", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7039", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2044150359694946789", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "493", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$CRDO management call summary on photonics M&A

Key Financial Information

Revenue guidance. 

Combined optical revenue (DSPs + ZeroFlap + standalone PICs) now expected to exceed $500M in FY27 — above prior guidance. Total FY27 revenue expected to grow in excess of 75% YoY. Sequencing: mid-single-digit sequential growth through H1, with a second-half inflection driven by the optical portfolio ramp.

Margins preserved. 

ZeroFlap optics stays within the long-term gross margin model in the mid-60s percent. Integrating the Dust PIC into ZeroFlap is margin accretive — eliminates third-party component margin stack. No shift in margin messaging despite the hardware mix shift toward transceivers.

Deal structure. 

Acquisition price not disclosed. Two-year contingency earnout based on a mix of financial metrics. Expected to close Q2 calendar 2026. Accretive in FY27 and beyond. Full OpEx and CapEx impact to be detailed at June earnings.

What Could Be Market Moving

The $500M optical number is new and above prior expectations. This reframes CRDO from a one-product AEC story into a diversified connectivity platform with a second major revenue pillar scaling fast. The Street will need to re-model FY27 upward.

75%+ total revenue growth guide is aggressive. That’s a step-up from where consensus likely sat pre-deal, and it’s being delivered while holding margin structure. Growth-at-scale with margin durability is exactly what commands premium multiples.

Standalone PIC TAM opens a new addressable market. Brennan cited analyst estimates of $6B by 2030. This wasn’t in anyone’s CRDO model before today. Dust’s backlog is “quite healthy and growing through FY27” — that’s real near-term revenue, not a roadmap promise.

3.2T readiness. Dust is already making progress at 448 Gbps per lane, ahead of the broader market. This positions CRDO as the enabling platform for the next port speed cycle before competitors are ready.
Tone of the Call

Confident and controlled. Brennan was deliberate about framing this as a natural extension of the system-level strategy rather than a pivot. No defensiveness on the margin question — Fleming handled it cleanly with no hedging. They declined to break out Dust’s standalone revenue or give granular optical segment splits, which signals they want to keep optionality on how they frame the narrative at June earnings.

The Q&A was constructive. Analysts were probing for risk (customer conflict on standalone PICs, margin dilution, qualification disruption for ZeroFlap) and management batted every one away without hesitation. Brennan’s repeated emphasis on “system-level approach” and “owning more of the stack” is the language of a company playing offense, not reacting to competitive pressure.
No sandbagging. 

The 75%+ growth and $500M optical numbers were offered proactively, not dragged out by analysts. That’s a management team that wanted the market to re-rate the story today.

text:
As of April 14, 2026 (removed some players from the list)

The AI photonics and optical infrastructure sector is showing a sharp split today, with substrate and materials plays surging while early-stage and speculative names get hammered. $SOI led the watchlist with an +11.91% move, extending a parabolic run fueled by accelerating investor conviction in Soitec's Photonics-SOI substrate business for co-packaged optics and AI datacenter interconnects. Morningstar noted that Soitec's shares rose 50% in just five trading sessions as investors priced in a bullish outlook for the firm's silicon photonics segment. Morningstar (https://t.co/djE1rX2T9F)
The broader sector is being reshaped by a clear bifurcation: capital is rotating into names with tangible substrate and test infrastructure exposure to the AI optical buildout, while pre-revenue photonic integration plays are getting punished. $POET dropped after Wolfpack Research disclosed a short position, calling the company "an obvious stock promotion" and flagging potential PFIC tax liabilities for U.S. shareholders ahead of the April 15 filing deadline. https://t.co/RRfdqNGIy4 $SIVEF fell in sympathy as the short report reinforced broader skepticism toward pre-revenue photonic chip names. Meanwhile, SPX +2.21% and NDX +1.66% confirm this is a risk-on tape where the photonics laggards are underperforming on idiosyncratic, not macro, weakness.

$SOI, $BESIY, $TSEM, $AXTI: The substrate tier is where capital is concentrating. SOI's parabolic move reflects the market treating Photonics-SOI wafers as a structural bottleneck for CPO adoption. BESIY (BE Semiconductor) rode the same wave on die-attach and advanced packaging exposure. TSEM continues to benefit from specialty foundry demand, while AXTI held green on InP substrate scarcity narrative despite thin volume.
$IQE, $GFS: IQE gave back -2.65% as the OTC liquidity drag persists despite the same end-market tailwinds. GFS slipped -1.28%, still digesting the auto/industrial overhang even as its RF-SOI partnership with Soitec positions it well longer term.
2. Optical Transceivers and Active Networking
$COHR, $MRVL: Coherent and Marvell both green, tracking the 800G/1.6T transceiver cycle. COHR +1.80% is steady rather than explosive here, which suggests the easy money in pluggable transceiver names may already be priced.
$LITE, $CIEN, $AAOI: The transceiver and networking names that should be benefiting from the same AI optical build are instead lagging. LITE -2.15% and CIEN -2.68% are giving back recent gains. AAOI -4.41% continues to trade heavy, potentially reflecting profit-taking after its tariff-exemption-driven run.
$GLW, $VIAV: Passive optical infrastructure names drifting lower. Not broken, just not where the heat is today.
3. Semiconductor Equipment, Test and Infrastructure
$AEHR, $FORM, $KEYS, $TER, $ATEYY: AEHR +3.84% is the standout, continuing its recent strength on wafer-level burn-in demand tied to SiC and compound semi production ramps. ATEYY (Advantest) surged +8.65%, a strong signal that test infrastructure spending for advanced packaging and photonics ICs is accelerating. FORM flat, KEYS and TER slightly red but neither signaling anything concerning.
4. Early-Stage Photonic Materials and PICs
$LWLG, $ALMU, $POET, $SIVEF: LWLG bucked the early-stage selloff with a +7.20% move, potentially on renewed interest in EO polymer modulators as CPO architectures demand novel modulator solutions beyond conventional LiNbO3. ALMU -7.36% gave back gains on thin float dynamics. POET -8.01% is the direct casualty of the Wolfpack short report, which weaponized the PFIC tax angle right before the April 15 deadline. SIVEF -11.50% is the worst performer on the board, dragged down by contagion from the short seller thesis hitting the pre-revenue photonic integration cohort.

TSEM, GFS, SOI, ATEYY, FPLSF: TSEM steady at +2.89% on SiPho foundry momentum. Rest positive but muted.

text:
The rotation into CPO names is inevitable. From what I am reading, it may be coming faster than most expect.

Soitec $SLOIF up 10% today. It is the only company on earth supplying the substrate TSMC, GlobalFoundries, STMicro, and Intel use for silicon photonics at volume. There is no second source.

Every pluggable transceiver shipping into AI data centers today uses 50mm2 of it. Every CPO engine uses 200mm2. Same monopoly. 4x the content per device.

The CPO market goes from essentially nothing today to $15 billion by 2030 (263% CARG). Soitec's Photonics-SOI revenue is already approaching €86 million in FY26 growing 25% annually. That is before the CPO ramp.

We published a full breakdown of where Soitec sits in the CPO supply chain and what the content step-up means for the next two years. Link in reply.

$SOI $TSEM $MRVL $LITE $COHR $AAOI

text:
The Photonics Ecosystem: Explained

Every segment, group, and notable stock within below. you'll want to bookmark this...

UPSTREAM

- Materials: $GLW $AXTI $ALMU $LWLG
- Wafer: $TSM $GFS $TSEM $SKWT 

MIDSTREAM 

- Optical Components: $HIMX $LPTH $OPTX $MTSI
- Lasers & Light: $LITE $IPGP $COHR $LASR
- Transceivers: $AAOI (+ others) 
- Packaging: $FN $AMKR $POET 

DOWNSTREAM 

- Switch & Interconnect: $AVGO $MRVL $CRDO $NVDA
- Networking: $JNPR $CIEN $CSCO $ANET 
- Testing: $AEHR $ONTO $VIAV $FORM 

I made this tool to help me understand the segmentation of the photonics world. I am not a photonics expert. If I missed anything, or need to move something around, let me know!

text:
Healthy rotation, the weakness you see in Photonics & Space themes happens when the leaders led the charge last week and were very extended.

That's why having a balanced PF and not be overly concentrated in one or a few groups is key to handle these normal & healthy rotations.
